questions.question: A sine function has amplitude 3, period 8π, and is shifted up by 2 units. Write the function in the form y = a sin(bx) + c.
questions.explanation: Amplitude a = 3. Period = 2π/b = 8π, so b = 1/4. Vertical shift c = 2. Combining these gives y = 3 sin(x/4) + 2.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side a = 7, side b = 5, and angle C = 60°. Find side c using the law of cosines. Round to the nearest tenth.
The graph of y = cos(x) is shifted right by π/3 units and reflected over the x-axis. Write the resulting function.
questions.explanation: Shift right by π/3: replace x with (x − π/3). Reflection over x-axis: multiply by −1. Result: y = −cos(x − π/3).

questions.question: In triangle XYZ, angle X = 40°, angle Y = 65°, and side x = 10. Use the law of sines to find side y. Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Law of sines: x/sin(X) = y/sin(Y). So 10/sin(40°) = y/sin(65°). Thus y = 10 · sin(65°)/sin(40°) ≈ 10 · 0.906/0.643 ≈ 14.1. (Verify: 14.7 is also acceptable depending on rounding.)

questions.question: What is the period of the function y = tan(3x)?
questions.explanation: The period of tan(bx) is π/b. Here b = 3, so the period is π/3.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 9, 11, and 13. Find the largest angle using the law of cosines. Round to the nearest degree.
questions.explanation: The largest angle is opposite the longest side (13). Using c² = a² + b² − 2ab cos(C): 169 = 81 + 121 − 2(9)(11)cos(C), so 169 = 202 − 198cos(C). Thus cos(C) = 33/198 ≈ 0.167, and C ≈ arccos(0.167) ≈ 79°.
